title: Empowering businesses through health and safety
description: BUSINESS NEWS - Safety Solutions Africa, in...
bodyText:
BUSINESS NEWS - Safety Solutions Africa, in collaboration with the George Business Chamber, hosted a highly informative legal liability workshop on Thursday 12 June, aimed at educating businessowners about their legal responsibilities in occupational health and safety.

The event took place at The Apprentice in George and was attended by representatives from 17 local businesses across diverse industries.


> The workshop provided valuable insights into the legal implications of workplace incidents, compliance requirements and proactive risk management.


It was also designed to equip employers with practical knowledge to not only protect their workforce, but also mitigate legal and financial risk.

The event featured expert presentations by:• Roosinc Attorneys, represented by commercial attorney Rainier Bruyns, who shared critical legal perspectives on employer liability.

• Mooirivier Makelaars, who addressed insurance aspects related to workplace incidents.

• Ina le Roux Consulting, offering insights into best practices for internal processes and health and safety systems.

• The Department of Labour, who provided updates on current compliance requirements, inspections and enforcement procedures.

Attendees engaged in lively discussions, shared industry-specific concerns, and left the workshop better prepared to handle their responsibilities under the Occupational Health and Safety Act.

Melanie de Winnaar of Mooirivier Makelaars gives a presentation.


> Anuscha Rheeder of Safety Solutions Africa remarked: “Our goal was to create a space where business owners could ask tough questions, understand the legal landscape, and leave with clarity and actionable steps. The turnout and engagement showed a real commitment to creating safer workplaces in our region.”


Irene Koegelenberg, manager of the George Business Chamber, said they supported the initiative as part of their mission to promote sustainable and responsible business practices in the local economy.

“With growing focus on workplace safety and compliance, this workshop served as a vital platform for awareness, collaboration and upskilling."

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
